revision.text:
[[ja:穴澤天神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Anazawa Tenjinsha | native_name = 穴澤天神社 | image = Anazawatenjin.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Anazawa Tenjinsha | coordinates = {{coord|35.632777777777775|139.52244444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|ca|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|fr|Sukunabikona|it|Sukuna-hikona|ja|スクナビコナ|ko|스쿠나비코나|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯕꯤꯀꯣꯅ|pl|Sukunabikona|pt|Sukunahikona|simple|Sukunabikona|sv|Suku-Na-Bikona|zh|少彥名|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }} {{nihongo|'''Anazawa Tenjinsha'''|穴澤天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * shrines|zh|天滿宮|qid=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11546334|ltq=Q11546334|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 9 ** P155: territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q317847|ltq=Q317847|qid=Q317847|1=Inagi}} * {{ill|Musashi Province|lt=Musashi Province|ar|محافظة موساشي|ca|Província de Musashi|ckb|پارێزگای موساشی|cs|Provincie Musaši|de|Provinz Musashi|en|Musashi Province|eo|Provinco Musaŝi|es|Provincia de Musashi|fa|ولایت موساشی|fr|Province de Musashi|gl|Provincia de Musashi|hak|Musashi-koet|id|Provinsi Musashi|it|Musashi (provincia)|ja|武蔵国|ko|무사시국|lt|Musašio žemė|nl|Musashi (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Musashi|pt|Província de Musashi|ru|Мусаси (провинция)|simple|Musashi Province|sk|Musaši (provincia)|sr|Провинција Мусаши|th|แคว้นมูซาชิ|tr|Musashi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Мусаші|vi|Musashi (tỉnh)|zh_min_nan|Musasi-kok|zh_yue|武藏國|zh|武藏國|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q930545|ltq=Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あなざわてんじんしゃ * アナサハノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == different from (P1889) == * {{ill|Q135069520|ltq=Q135069520|qid=Q135069520|1=Anazawa Tenjin Shrine}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4013405000238 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anasahano- Shrine|lt=Anasahano- Shrine|qid=Q135039729|1=Anasahano- Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都稲城市矢野口3292', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:穴澤天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11597242}} == Japanese content == {{Source citation needed|date=January 2014}} {{Shinto shrine | Name=Anazawa Tenjin Shrine | Image=[[File:Anazawatenjin.JPG|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inagi City|1=UNKNOWN}}3292 Yanokuchi{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Association}} | Coordinates={{Coord|35|37|58|N|139|31|20.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Association}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor) candidate shrine
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Association}} | Founded=(traditional) 4th year of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Association}} | Annual festival={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=August 25|1=UNKNOWN}} | Main hall={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Anazawa Tenjin Shrine|position=left|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} }} '''Anazawa Tenjin Shrine''' (あなざわてんじんしゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yanokuchi (Inagi City)|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inagi City|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is identified with “Anazawa Shrine of Tama District, Musashi Province” listed in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Primary deity''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Association}} : Although the current primary enshrined deity is Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, there are differing opinions; it is also thought that originally the local tutelary deity of the area (the Anazawa deity) may have been worshipped instead. '''Associated deities''' * Kan-shin ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=UNKNOWN}}){{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Association}}{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=435}} : In the 7th year of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Genroku|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=1694|1=UNKNOWN}}), the enshrined deity of Tenman Shrine was merged. * Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Association}} : In the 7th year of Taishō (1918), the enshrined deity of Kuniyasu Shrine was merged. === Subordinate shrines === * Inari Shrine * Shinmei Shrine * Sannō Shrine * Benten Shrine : Until the Edo period, Buddhist statues such as Daikokuten and Bishamonten were placed at the entrance of the cave{{sfn|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki, Yanokuchi Village}}, but in the 4th year of Meiji (1871) they were relocated to the former bettō temple, Ikō-ji{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=435}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benten Cave|1=UNKNOWN}})[https://www.city.inagi.tokyo.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai_note.files/BENten-DOkutsu.pdf Cultural Property Note No.45: Benten Cave] Inagi City Board of Education, accessed April 14, 2023. Today, a stone shrine remains in the cave on the left. === Overview === [[File:穴澤天神社の湧水.jpg|thumb|200px|Spring water]] The shrine is said to have been founded in March of the 4th year of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=423 BC|1=UNKNOWN}}){{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=438}}. The precincts are located on the mid-slope of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tama Hills|1=UNKNOWN}} along the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Misawa River|1=UNKNOWN}}. From a cave shrine beneath the cliff directly below the precincts, spring water selected as one of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=57 Famous Springs of Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}} flows forth, and there is no shortage of people who come to draw this water. According to the “Anazawa Tenjin Engi,” in the 7th year of Genroku ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=1694|1=UNKNOWN}}), the local land steward Katō Tarōzaemon renovated the shrine buildings and merged Tenman Shrine. On the slope on the north side of the precincts (toward the Misawa River) there are horizontal caves where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=stone Buddhas|1=UNKNOWN}} were once enshrined, but they have now been moved into the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benten Cave|1=UNKNOWN}} of Ikō-ji Temple. It is unclear whether this action was the result of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=anti-Buddhist movement|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine buildings were restored in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=1986|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within the precincts stands a commemorative monument honoring the achievements of the private academy “Keigi-juku” run by Kubo Zenryō, a Confucian scholar said to have named the place Inagi. The grand annual festival is held on {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=August 25|1=UNKNOWN}}, during which {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo no Sato Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} (a nationally designated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}}{{National Designated Cultural Properties Database|302|44|Edo no Sato Kagura}}) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lion dances|1=UNKNOWN}} (a city-designated cultural property{{sfn|Inagi City}}) are offered. === Access === * 5 minutes on foot from “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Keiō Yomiuriland Station|1=UNKNOWN}}” on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Keiō Sagamihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. * 20 minutes on foot from “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yanokuchi Station|1=UNKNOWN}}” on the JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nambu Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/inagi/6070/|title=Anazawa Tenjin Shrine|accessdate=2020-7-13|publisher=Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Association|ref=harv}} * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}|volume=Volume 95, Tama District No.7|chapter=Yanokuchi Village, Anazawa Tenjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/44}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau of the Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki, Yanokuchi Village}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.inagi.tokyo.jp/kanko/rekishi/inagishibunkazai/inagi_bunkazai/sisimai.html|title=Lion Dance (Shishimai)|accessdate=2020-7-13|publisher=Inagi City|ref=harv}} * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/222}}|publisher=Yuhōdō Shoten|editor=Nagaaki Saitō|volume=2|series=Yuhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Volume 3, Tenkino-bu, Anazawa Tenjin Shrine|pages=435–438|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} === External links === * [http://anazawatenjinja.jp/ Anazawa Tenjin Shrine] (official website) == Categories ==
